headline: Prompt engineering surged in momentum while the agent orchestration stack cooled across the month.
body: Prompt engineering broke higher with momentum at 145, far ahead of a softening orchestration layer where orchestration, agent roles, subagents, and workflow orchestration all weakened or lagged alongside Model Context Protocol and Language Server Protocol. A cluster of new technique entrants appeared, including cost analysis, image generation API, flow matching, automated testing, quality control, and a decision trees breakout. Interface direction was in play as OpenAI signaled a move past chat toward a super app [3], while practitioner anxiety surfaced around LLMs reshaping software careers [8] and the differing ROI of AI coding for bootstrapped founders [5]. Tooling requests and learning use cases also drew attention, from calls for a Linux Claude Desktop [7] to LLM-generated tutorials [11].

cited_items.title: OpenAI is still working on that ‘super app’
The ROI of AI coding looks different when you are a bootstrapped founder
Anthropic, please ship an official Claude Desktop for Linux
LLMs are eroding my software engineering career and I don't know what to do
Show HN: Lathe – Use LLMs to learn a new domain, not skip past it
